title: Earthy Velvety German Onion Dumplings
description: A comforting brunch dish of fluffy potato-and-onion dumplings with a silky, caramelized finish, perfect for a relaxed weekend breakfast.

ingredients.name: Russet potatoes
ingredients.notes: peeled, boiled until tender, then mashed until smooth

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.notes: finely diced, sautéed in a little butter until caramelized

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: combined with baking powder for lift

instructions: Boil the potatoes until fork‑soft, drain, and mash until silky.
In a skillet, melt a tablespoon of butter and sauté the diced onion over medium heat until deep golden and fragrant, about 8–10 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, parsley, nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
Add the mashed potatoes, sautéed onions, egg, and milk to the dry ingredients, mixing until a cohesive dough forms; if too wet, add a tablespoon more flour.
Knead the dough gently on a lightly floured surface for 2–3 minutes until smooth; it should feel supple but not sticky.
Let the dough rest at room temperature for 10 minutes to relax the gluten.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and shape each into a round dumpling about 3 cm in diameter.
Heat the remaining melted butter in a non‑stick skillet over medium heat; place the dumplings in the pan, cooking 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and puffed.
Transfer the cooked dumplings to a serving platter, sprinkle with fresh chives, and drizzle with a little extra melted butter for shine.
Serve immediately with a side of creamy mustard or a light yogurt dip for a velvety finish.
